methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $5.0B.

data.narrative:
Modern Hydrogen, a Series B climate and energy company founded by Tony Pan, has raised $100M to date. The valuation analysis draws on two primary triangulation methods applied against a comp universe of six private peers at identical stage within the Climate & Energy sector, supplemented by one public comparable.

The stage-progression method applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement to the $100M total raised proxy, implying a valuation of $190M. The peer-median approach benchmarks Modern Hydrogen against six same-sector, same-stage companies—including Commonwealth Fusion Systems ($5.0B), Pano AI ($500M), Sage Geosystems ($400M), Reverion ($310M), and CarbonCapture Inc. ($300M)—yielding a median peer valuation of $355M. The peer range spans $120M to $5.0B, reflecting meaningful dispersion driven partly by Commonwealth Fusion Systems' outlier valuation.

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $162M (low) to $408M (high), with a median of $272M. The substantial spread reflects sector-wide uncertainty: early-stage climate tech valuations remain volatile and dependent on technology differentiation, regulatory environment assumptions, and capital availability. The peer cohort itself demonstrates this tension—a 41x spread from minimum to maximum. Key uncertainties include Modern Hydrogen's specific technology maturity relative to peers and the sustainability of current climate-tech multiples in the event of capital market contraction.
